scenes.description: Hero title introduces the paradox: KHK is one enzyme with two jobs, using a clean blue-white scientific title treatment.
scenes.framing: Centered hero title with molecule and hepatocyte labels implied through typography and icon-like text badges.
scenes.movement: Fade-up title, subtle scale-in, two supporting labels slide in from opposite sides.
scenes.overlay_notes: Title: KHK: one enzyme, two jobs. Lower note: fructose metabolism + stress adaptation.
scenes.shot_intent: Stop the scroll with the paper's paradox before introducing pathway details.
scenes.information_role: Viewer learns KHK will be framed as both metabolic and protective.

required_assets.description: HeroTitle cut rendered with Remotion; no external media required.

scenes.description: Nutrient-overload pathway diagram: fructose plus saturated fat enter hepatocyte and create ER folding backlog.
scenes.framing: Full-screen process diagram with three rounded nodes arranged left-to-right.
scenes.movement: Nodes appear progressively: nutrients, ER overload, adapt-or-die fork.
scenes.overlay_notes: Key term card: ER proteostasis = keeping protein folding under control.
scenes.shot_intent: Translate dense ER-stress terminology into a simple decision pathway.
scenes.information_role: Viewer learns why nutrient stress creates a cell-fate problem.

required_assets.description: Progress-bar/diagram cut representing nutrient overload to ER backlog to cell-fate fork.

scenes.description: Split-screen comparison of KHK present versus KHK knockdown: adaptive UPR on the left, proteotoxic/apoptotic stress on the right.
scenes.framing: Two-column comparison with strong left/right contrast and clear labels.
scenes.movement: Left side fills blue for adaptive branch, then right side fills amber/red for aggregate/apoptosis branch.
scenes.overlay_notes: Contrast card: KHK present: adaptive UPR; KHK loss: proteotoxic stress.
scenes.shot_intent: Make the central experimental contrast legible in one visual beat.
scenes.information_role: Viewer learns the core finding: KHK presence changes the stress-signaling outcome.

required_assets.description: ComparisonCard cut contrasting adaptive IRE1α/XBP1 with proteotoxic/apoptotic stress.

scenes.description: Central aha diagram showing metabolism and ER proteostasis intersecting at KHK.
scenes.framing: Centered Venn/intersection-style layout with KHK as the connecting node.
scenes.movement: Two streams converge into KHK, then a protective ER proteostasis label pulses once.
scenes.overlay_notes: Aha card: Metabolism ↔ ER stress adaptation.
scenes.shot_intent: Deliver the conceptual payoff of the video in a single memorable diagram.
scenes.information_role: Viewer learns the paper reframes KHK as a bridge between metabolism and proteostasis.

required_assets.description: Callout and progress visuals showing metabolism and proteostasis meeting at KHK.

scenes.description: Therapeutic balance: reducing harmful fructose flux must be balanced against preserving ER protection.
scenes.framing: Two-sided balance board with left and right therapeutic considerations.
scenes.movement: Scale settles into balance; warning chip appears for complete deletion.
scenes.shot_intent: Turn the biological finding into the translational takeaway.
scenes.information_role: Viewer learns the therapeutic nuance: selective inhibition may matter.

required_assets.description: Comparison/progress cut showing reduce fructose flux versus preserve ER protection.

scenes.description: CTA end card with DOI and a concise summary message.
scenes.framing: Centered DOI and concise final message with generous whitespace.
scenes.movement: CTA fades in, DOI underlines with accent color.
scenes.shot_intent: End with an exact, readable call to action.
scenes.information_role: Viewer knows where to find the paper.

required_assets.description: TextCard CTA rendered exactly in Remotion.

metadata.auto_advanced_reason: User requested making the video without additional human-in-loop steps.

decorative_visuals.reason: Most scenes have clear communicative purpose
